headline: Home-Based Palliative Care Serves Only 4,200 Patients Despite 52,000 Need
context: Community-based end-of-life care reaches a small fraction of terminal patients who prefer to die at home.

cards.big: Only 4,200 of 52,000 terminal patients receive home-based palliative care in 2025
cards.sub: 92% of terminal patients express preference to die at home but only 8% are able to

cards.big: Government home palliative care teams exist in only 22 of 154 health districts
cards.sub: Coverage gap forces most terminal patients into hospital care against their wishes

cards.big: Home-based palliative care costs RM4,500 per patient versus RM28,000 for hospital end-of-life care
cards.sub: Community model is 84% cheaper while producing higher patient and family satisfaction

cards.big: Australia's home palliative care program reaches 72% of terminal patients through community nursing
cards.sub: Scaled community model achieved majority coverage through dedicated funding stream

cards.big: Forcing people to die in hospitals when they want to die at home is a failure of compassion and economics
Should home palliative care be available in every health district as a standard service
